calendar.matters:
Objections To Final Discharge
Off Calendar Jun-29-2016 Denied

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Cynthia Schreurs for and with (administrator) Chinetta Cade. Nicholas Jellins specially appearing for Guilio Accornero for (objector) Etta Anderson. Ronald Paige in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it has objections limited to the request for fees and asked if there were any other issues. Mr. Jellins stated that that is all he is aware of. Mr. Paige stated that he is not here to object. The Court stated that there is an issue regarding the statutory fees, that it is premised on the administrator having either not been truthful or made mistakes, issues that go with that are whether the court has authority to reduce statutory fees on that basis, whether the fee was waived by the 4/20 filing, if the waiver was properly withdrawn, if the ex parte resolved issues, that it is not sure that waiving a request for fees prevents asking for them at another time and that it does not think the ex parte resolved this. Ms. Schreurs stated that she thought the ex parte resolved the issue, that they presented oral argument and questioned witnesses. The Court explained that ex partes are not designed to decide these types of issues and that there is nothing in the order suggesting wrong doing or sanctions. Ms. Schreurs reported that it was a mistake by the administrator, that at the time of death of her nephew the family members made it hard for her and that she had to keep proving that she was a family member. The Court stating that it was not sure how that had anything to do with omitting siblings from the matter. Mr. Jellin stated that the pleadings outline all relevant matters including the administrators failure to name her own sister as an heir. Ms. Schreurs stated that no one has asked for her removal, that she had done the work and should get the statutory fees. Mr. Jellin stated that it created additional costs to the beneficiaries and that they had to appear at 3 hearings. Mr. Jellin stated that imposing sanctions for dishonesty/omissions would be proper. Ms. Schreurs stated that the administrator has done her job, is 81 years old and that it was difficult to get around. The court took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
